notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix → /nw-deliver. The authoritative specification is the RCA at `docs/feature/fix-convergence-loop-not-spawned/bugfix-rca.md` (user-reviewed and APPROVED 2026-04-28 in Phase 2 before roadmap creation). Approved fix shape: **Option B2 — broker-driven §18 wiring**, NOT B1 (IntentStore-scan polling). B1 was explicitly rejected as throwaway intermediate code that contradicts whitepaper §18 *Triggering Model — Hybrid by Design* and would be deleted when the broker wiring lands properly.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: B2 lands directly (not B1 then B2). No deprecation, no shadow-spawned loop, no feature-flagged old path. The 7 production edits in Step 01-02 ship as one cohesive commit.

Two-step Outside-In TDD shape, mirroring the canonical bugfix template at `docs/feature/fix-restart-backoff-deadline-not-written/deliver/roadmap.json`. Step 01-01 writes the failing regression test (RED — committed with --no-verify per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §RED scaffolds). Step 01-02 lands the cohesive 7-part fix; the RED test transitions RED → GREEN within the same commit.

RED via compile failure (preferred shape). Step 01-01's new test references `ServerConfig.clock` and `ServerConfig.tick_cadence` directly — fields that do NOT yet exist on `ServerConfig` against current main (`crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:137-188`). The compile failure IS the RED state — it pins the exact fields the GREEN step must publish AND prevents the crafter from making the test compile by adding shadow types. Same shape used by the prior `fix-restart-backoff-deadline-not-written` bugfix (which used a missing constant as the compile-failure pin). Documented up front so the DES PREPARE/RED phases handle the compile-error state cleanly.

Test placement: NEW file at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot.rs`, registered through the existing `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration.rs` entrypoint's `mod integration { mod job_lifecycle { ... } }` block (alongside the existing `submit_to_running`, `crash_recovery`, `stop_to_terminated` siblings). Per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §"Layout — integration tests live under `tests/integration/`": the `#![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` gate is on the entrypoint (`tests/integration.rs:13`), NOT this new file. The new file gets a plain `// SimClock + SimDriver — runs on macOS in default lane` comment, no cfg attribute.

SimClock + SimDriver lane (runs on macOS in the default `--features integration-tests` lane). Unlike the three existing convergence tests (`submit_to_running.rs`, `crash_recovery.rs`, `stop_to_terminated.rs`) which are `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` because they exercise real `ProcessDriver` cgroup writes, this regression test deliberately uses `Arc<SimDriver::new(DriverType::Process)>` so it runs uniformly on macOS dev hosts and Linux CI alike. The test still goes through `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` end-to-end (real bound HTTPS listener, real rustls, real reqwest, real `LocalIntentStore` on tempdir) — closing the seam all 5 existing real-server tests leave open.

Existing-test risk to flag for the crafter (load-bearing — DO NOT MASK): under SimDriver + SimClock the 5 real-server tests (`submit_round_trip`, `describe_round_trip`, `idempotent_resubmit`, `concurrent_submit_toctou`, `server_lifecycle`, `observation_empty_rows`) currently boot through `run_server` (which uses real `ProcessDriver` + real `SystemClock`). After the fix, the convergence loop spawns under their `..Default::default()`-constructed `ServerConfig`. Behavioural impact under SimDriver is none (the loop is a no-op as long as no one enqueues an Evaluation). However, `submit_round_trip.rs` DOES submit a job via the new `enqueue` path — after the fix, those tests will see the broker dispatch and SimDriver will record a Running alloc table. That is correct behaviour and the tests must be audited to confirm they don't accidentally assert on a quiescent state. Per the user-rule applied to test breakage: address each affected test in this PR, never mask. The Lima-VM run inside the GREEN step's quality gate is the surface for catching these.

Broker mutability gap discovered during architecture analysis (must be flagged to crafter). `ReconcilerRuntime::broker(&self) -> &EvaluationBroker` (line 121) returns an immutable reference, but `EvaluationBroker::submit(&mut self, eval: Evaluation)` (line 87) and `EvaluationBroker::drain_pending(&mut self) -> Vec<Evaluation>` (line 98) both require `&mut self`. The handler-side enqueue path (`state.runtime.broker().submit(...)`) and the spawned-loop drain path (`state.runtime.broker().drain_pending()`) both go through `Arc<ReconcilerRuntime>` (line 83), so neither has `&mut`. The crafter must EITHER (a) introduce interior mutability around the broker (e.g. `broker: Mutex<EvaluationBroker>`) and update `broker()` / `counters()` accessor sites accordingly, or (b) add a separate `broker_mut()` accessor that returns a guard. Option (a) is the recommended path because `cluster_status` already calls `state.runtime.broker().counters()` and that read must continue to work; wrapping the broker in `parking_lot::Mutex` keeps the lock cheap and panic-free per `.claude/rules/development.md` ("Use `parking_lot::RwLock` / `Mutex` over `std::sync::RwLock` / `Mutex` for synchronous critical sections") AND no `.await` is held across the lock (the broker's methods are sync and the convergence-loop holds the guard only long enough to drain into a `Vec<Evaluation>`). Document the choice in the GREEN commit body.

Broker API name correction. RCA §Approved fix item 4 says "call `state.runtime.broker().enqueue(...)`". The actual method on `EvaluationBroker` (verified at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/eval_broker.rs:87`) is `pub fn submit(&mut self, eval: Evaluation)`, not `enqueue`. The roadmap below uses `submit` to match the actual API surface. The `Evaluation` constructor is also a struct literal `Evaluation { reconciler, target }`, not `Evaluation::new(...)` — there is no `::new` on `Evaluation` (verified at `eval_broker.rs:53-57`). Both rename + literal-vs-constructor are crafter-facing details that the RCA elided.

Reconciler name registered in the runtime: `"job-lifecycle"` (with hyphen) — verified at `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:1062` (`ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle")`). The `submit_job` / `stop_job` handler calls MUST use this exact name when constructing the `Evaluation`; a typo collapses the broker key collapse to per-typo silos and breaks the §18 contract.

`run_convergence_tick` self-re-enqueue (RCA §Approved fix item 5). When `actions.len() > 0`, the function must call `state.runtime.broker().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: name.clone(), target: target.clone() })` so the next tick re-evaluates. This is the level-triggered §18 half. The function currently takes `state: &AppState` (line 165) which already has `state.runtime.broker()` access — but the `&mut`-vs-`&` mutability gap above applies here too. Once the broker has interior mutability, the self-re-enqueue is one line per actions-emitted iteration.

CLI wiring (RCA §Approved fix item 7). The `overdrive serve` call site is `crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/serve.rs:101-126` (the `pub async fn run(args: ServeArgs) -> Result<ServeHandle, CliError>` body). It currently constructs `ServerConfig { bind, data_dir, operator_config_dir, allow_no_cgroups }` (lines 104-109) and calls `run_server(config)`. After the field additions, this call site must populate `clock: Arc::new(overdrive_host::SystemClock)` and `tick_cadence: overdrive_control_plane::reconciler_runtime::DEFAULT_TICK_CADENCE` (or omit and let `Default` carry them). Per CLAUDE.md "Repository structure", `overdrive-host` is the only crate permitted to instantiate `SystemClock`, so this is the correct boundary. `overdrive-cli/Cargo.toml` already depends on `overdrive-control-plane`; add an `overdrive-host` workspace dep entry if not already present.

Mutation testing scope (post-GREEN, run with `run_in_background: true` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing exception): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests`. Kill-rate gate ≥80%. The two assertions in the regression test (`info.broker.dispatched >= 1` and `allocs.iter().any(|a| a.state == AllocState::Running)`) jointly kill the three Root Cause classes: A (spawn missing → no dispatch + no Running), B (handler doesn't enqueue → no dispatch even after submit), and C (broker counter never advances → dispatched stays 0). Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome.

Standing user-rule references the crafter must obey: (1) `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations` — no shims, no deprecations, no two-phase rollout. (2) `feedback_no_git_stash` — focused-subset commits use `git add <paths>` + `git restore --staged`, never `git stash`. (3) `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer` — clippy issues that surface during the work get fixed in-scope. (4) `feedback_no_rename_stubs` — no leftover stubs. (5) `feedback_delete_dont_gate` — no `#[cfg(test)]` annotations to silence dead-code warnings on production code. (6) `.claude/rules/development.md` §Reconciler I/O — the spawn loop runs in a wiring crate (`overdrive-control-plane` is `crate_class = adapter-host`) where direct `Clock::now`/`Clock::sleep` via the injected trait is allowed; the dst-lint gate scans only `core` crates. (7) `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running tests — foreground, always" — every test invocation goes through the `Bash` tool with `run_in_background: false` except the post-GREEN mutation pass.

Roadmap-validator length warnings (`STEP_NAME_TOO_LONG`, `CRITERIA_TOO_LONG`, `DESCRIPTION_TOO_LONG`) are non-blocking per CLAUDE.md "Roadmap validator warnings" — the Overdrive convention deliberately carries scenario-level specificity in ACs (test names, broker method names, line numbers, gate predicates). Do NOT trim ACs to satisfy these warnings.

reviews.scope: RCA + proposed fix shape (Option B2: broker-driven §18 wiring with the 7 specific code changes; SimClock+SimDriver regression test gating broker.dispatched ≥ 1 AND alloc reaching Running through the bound HTTPS listener; existing real-server tests audited in-PR rather than masked).
reviews.notes: User confirmed root cause chain (A: production spawn missing; B: handlers don't enqueue; C: no automated gate on broker.dispatched). User APPROVED Option B2 specifically over B1 (IntentStore-scan polling) on the grounds that B1 contradicts whitepaper §18 and would be throwaway intermediate code. User APPROVED the regression-test design (SimClock+SimDriver, real-server boot, two-assertion shape) and APPROVED the in-PR audit scope for the 5 existing real-server tests. Out of scope explicitly: extending DST harness to drive the production server in-process (separate follow-up); promotion to multi-region or HA broker semantics.

phases.name: Spawn the convergence tick loop in the production server with broker-driven §18 wiring
phases.description: Two-step Outside-In TDD bugfix. Step 01-01 creates a NEW failing integration test at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot.rs` and registers it under the existing `tests/integration.rs` entrypoint's `mod integration { mod job_lifecycle { ... } }` block (lines 50-63). The test boots `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` end-to-end with `Arc<SimClock>` + `Arc<SimObservationStore>` + `Arc<SimDriver(DriverType::Process)>`, submits a 1-replica `payments` job via reqwest over the bound HTTPS listener, advances the SimClock 30 × 100ms with `tokio::task::yield_now()` between each tick, then asserts (a) `info.broker.dispatched >= 1` via `GET /v1/cluster/info` AND (b) at least one alloc reached `Running` via `GET /v1/allocs`. The test references `ServerConfig.clock` and `ServerConfig.tick_cadence` — fields that do NOT exist on `ServerConfig` against current main (`crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:137-188` shows only `bind`, `data_dir`, `operator_config_dir`, `allow_no_cgroups`). The compile failure on these field references IS the RED state. Step 01-02 lands the cohesive 7-part fix in one commit per Option B2: (1) `ServerConfig` adds `clock: Arc<dyn Clock>` and `tick_cadence: Duration` fields with `Default` impl carrying `Arc::new(SystemClock)` and `DEFAULT_TICK_CADENCE` from `reconciler_runtime.rs:144`. (2) `ServerHandle` adds `convergence_task: tokio::task::JoinHandle<()>` and `convergence_shutdown: tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken`; `shutdown(...)` ordering becomes cancel→await convergence task→axum graceful→await server_task. (3) `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` (`lib.rs:354`) spawns the convergence tick loop between `AppState::new` and listener bind, draining the broker each tick and dispatching `run_convergence_tick` per pending Evaluation. (4) `submit_job` (`handlers.rs:99-150`) and `stop_job` (`handlers.rs:267-297`) call `state.runtime.broker_mut().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle")?, target: TargetResource::new(format!("job/{job_id}"))? })` after the IntentStore write succeeds. (5) `run_convergence_tick` (`reconciler_runtime.rs:164-213`) self-re-enqueues `(reconciler_name, target)` when `actions.len() > 0` so the level-triggered §18 half holds. (6) The aspirational docstring at `reconciler_runtime.rs:155-158` is replaced with one that names `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` as the call site and references `ServerHandle::convergence_task` shutdown ordering. (7) `crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/serve.rs:104-109` constructs `clock: Arc::new(overdrive_host::SystemClock)` and `tick_cadence: DEFAULT_TICK_CADENCE` (or relies on `..Default::default()`) when populating `ServerConfig`. Plus a workspace-level `[workspace.dependencies]` entry for `tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["rt"] }` and `overdrive-control-plane/Cargo.toml` adding `tokio-util.workspace = true`. The broker-mutability adapter (interior mutability via `parking_lot::Mutex<EvaluationBroker>` or equivalent) is required because handlers and the spawn loop both go through `Arc<ReconcilerRuntime>` and need `&mut` on the broker — see roadmap notes for the recommended path. Step 01-01's regression test transitions RED → GREEN within Step 01-02's single commit. All existing tests must remain GREEN; per RCA §Risk, the 5 existing real-server tests get audited in-PR for behavioural compatibility under the new spawn behaviour. `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane`, `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck), full integration run inside Lima VM, `cargo xtask dst`, `cargo xtask dst-lint`, and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` all green.

steps.name: RED regression test — production server boot must spawn the convergence loop and dispatch broker-enqueued evaluations
steps.description: Create a NEW integration test file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot.rs` and register it under the existing `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration.rs` entrypoint's `mod integration { mod job_lifecycle { ... } }` block. The new file inherits the `#![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` gate from the entrypoint per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §"Layout — integration tests live under `tests/integration/`" — do NOT add a cfg attribute to the new file. The test fixture pattern follows `tests/integration/observation_empty_rows.rs` (which already boots `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` with `Arc<SimDriver(DriverType::Process)>` and a manually-constructed `Arc<dyn ObservationStore>` for the canary-injection defence). Helpers to import / mirror: `client_trusting(&ca_pem)` for the reqwest client trusting the ephemeral CA, `read_ca_from_trust_triple(&operator_config_dir)` for the trust-triple round-trip (both already in `submit_round_trip.rs` and `observation_empty_rows.rs`). New test function: `#[tokio::test] async fn submitted_job_reaches_running_via_real_server_boot() { ... }`. Body shape: (1) Create a `tempfile::TempDir`; subdirs `data` and `conf`. (2) Construct `let clock = Arc::new(overdrive_sim::adapters::clock::SimClock::new());` (cloning is share-state per `clock.rs:71-82`). (3) Construct `let obs: Arc<dyn ObservationStore> = Arc::new(SimObservationStore::single_peer(NodeId::new("local").expect("node id"), 0));`. (4) Construct `let driver: Arc<dyn Driver> = Arc::new(SimDriver::new(DriverType::Process));`. (5) Construct `ServerConfig { bind: "127.0.0.1:0".parse().unwrap(), data_dir, operator_config_dir, allow_no_cgroups: true, tick_cadence: Duration::from_millis(100), clock: clock.clone(), }` — note that `clock` and `tick_cadence` are the load-bearing field references that fail to compile against current main; this IS the RED state. (6) Call `let handle = run_server_with_obs_and_driver(config, obs.clone(), driver.clone()).await.expect("server boot");`. (7) Read CA via `read_ca_from_trust_triple(&operator_config_dir)` and build the reqwest client. (8) Submit the `payments` job via `POST /v1/jobs` with `JobSpecInput { id: "payments".to_string(), replicas: 1, cpu_milli: 100, memory_bytes: 256 * 1024 * 1024 }` (matching `submit_to_running.rs:60-66`'s shape); assert HTTP 200 + `IdempotencyOutcome::Inserted`. (9) Loop `for _ in 0..30 { clock.tick(Duration::from_millis(100)); tokio::task::yield_now().await; }` to advance the SimClock 30 × 100ms (matches the test's chosen tick_cadence of 100ms; gives the spawned loop 30 ticks of logical time). Note: the SimClock method is `tick(&self, duration: Duration)` not `advance(...)` — verified at `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/clock.rs:55`. (10) Assertion 1 (kills Root Cause C — broker counter never advances): `let info: ClusterStatus = client.get(format!("https://localhost:{}/v1/cluster/info", bound.port())).send().await?.json().await?;` then `assert!(info.broker.dispatched >= 1, "broker.dispatched must advance under steady-state traffic; got {}", info.broker.dispatched);`. (11) Assertion 2 (kills Roots A + B together — spawn missing + submit doesn't enqueue): `let allocs: AllocStatusResponse = client.get(format!("https://localhost:{}/v1/allocs", bound.port())).send().await?.json().await?;` then `assert!(allocs.rows.iter().any(|a| a.job_id == "payments" && a.state == "Running"), "submitted job must reach Running via the production convergence loop; got {:?}", allocs.rows);`. (12) Shutdown: `handle.shutdown(Duration::from_secs(1)).await;`. RED verification: `cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` errors with `unknown field clock in ServerConfig` and/or `unknown field tick_cadence in ServerConfig` (potentially also `unknown field clock` on `Default::default()`-rest-pattern construction depending on syntax). The compile failure IS the RED state — the crafter MUST NOT introduce a parallel `ServerConfig` shadow type, MUST NOT add the fields locally to make the test compile, and MUST NOT comment out the field references. The fields land in Step 01-02. The commit MUST be made with `git commit --no-verify` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §RED scaffolds; the commit body MUST cite that rule by name AND name Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart. No production-code edits in this step. Commit trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01`. Sub-task: append `mod convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot;` inside the `pub mod job_lifecycle { ... }` block at `tests/integration.rs:50-63` so the new file is reachable through the existing entrypoint.
steps.criteria: New file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot.rs` with one `#[tokio::test] async fn submitted_job_reaches_running_via_real_server_boot()` (and no other tests) created. The file body uses `ServerConfig { ..., tick_cadence: Duration::from_millis(100), clock: clock.clone(), ..Default::default() }` (or equivalent rest-pattern) — the literal `tick_cadence` and `clock` field names are the load-bearing RED pin. `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration.rs:50-63` is appended with `mod convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot;` inside `pub mod job_lifecycle { ... }` so the test is reachable from the entrypoint. The new file does NOT add `#![cfg(target_os = "linux")]` (SimDriver works on all platforms) and does NOT add `#![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` (entrypoint already gates the binary). RED verification: `cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` errors with `unknown field clock in ServerConfig` and/or `unknown field tick_cadence in ServerConfig` (NOT typos, NOT missing fixture imports). No production-code files touched (`git diff --name-only HEAD` shows only the new test file and the modified `tests/integration.rs`). No new feature flags introduced. Commit message: `test(reconciler): pin convergence loop must spawn under run_server_with_obs_and_driver (RED scaffold — see Step-ID: 01-01)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit uses `git commit --no-verify` and the body explicitly cites `.claude/rules/testing.md` §RED scaffolds, naming Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Symptom (closure pin) — the new test directly demonstrates the user-observable bug: against current main (with the production-spawn omission, the handler-doesn't-enqueue gap, and the absent gate on `broker.dispatched`), the test would fail at assertion 1 (`broker.dispatched == 0`) AND at assertion 2 (no Running alloc) IF the test were to compile. The compile failure pins the precondition for the runtime assertions: the production fields needed to even construct the test fixture do not exist yet.

RCA §5 Whys WHY 5 (root cause: production-side wiring of the convergence task never landed) — Step 01-01's RED state is precisely the absence of the configuration surface (`clock`, `tick_cadence`) that the production-side wiring requires. The compile error names the missing fields by name.

RCA §Approved fix items 1, 2 (the `ServerConfig` field additions are pinned by the test's field-reference syntax) — covered by the test's `tick_cadence: ..., clock: ...,` literal field references in the `ServerConfig { ... }` struct expression.
RCA §Approved fix item 3 (the convergence loop must run end-to-end and dispatch broker evaluations) — covered by assertion 1 (`info.broker.dispatched >= 1`) at runtime.

RCA §Approved fix item 4 (`submit_job` must enqueue an Evaluation) — covered jointly by assertions 1 + 2: assertion 1 fails if no Evaluation reaches the broker; assertion 2 fails if no Evaluation is dispatched into a `RestartAllocation`/`StartAllocation` action that drives the SimDriver state.

RCA §Approved fix item 5 (`run_convergence_tick` self-re-enqueue) — covered indirectly by assertion 2: a one-shot dispatch that emits a placement action but never re-enqueues to observe the resulting Pending → Running transition would fail to reach Running within 30 ticks. The test asserts on the Running terminal state, not just on the placement-action emission.

RCA §Regression test design — the test boots `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` end-to-end (closing the seam all 5 existing real-server tests leave open); uses SimClock + SimDriver (runs on macOS in default lane); asserts on both `broker.dispatched >= 1` (Root Cause C) AND alloc reaches `Running` (Roots A + B); today fails at compile, against the post-fix codebase passes.

Test placement per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §"Layout — integration tests live under `tests/integration/`": new file under `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/`, registered through the existing `tests/integration.rs` entrypoint's inline `mod integration { ... }` block. The `#![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` gate is on the entrypoint, NOT this new file (per the same section's submodule-cfg rule).

RED verified at the compile layer: `cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` errors with `unknown field clock` and/or `unknown field tick_cadence` on `ServerConfig`. Any other compile error (typo, missing fixture import, missing `mod convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot;`) is a step-author bug — fix and re-verify.

The crafter does NOT introduce a parallel `ServerConfig` shadow type, does NOT add the fields locally to make the test compile, and does NOT comment out the field references. The fields land in Step 01-02; the import is held to fail until then.
No production-code edits (production fix lands in 01-02). No new feature flags introduced. The only files modified are the new test file and the `tests/integration.rs` mod-declaration append.

Commit uses `git commit --no-verify` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §RED scaffolds; commit body explicitly cites the rule and names Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart. Commit message carries `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer.
steps.tier: Tier 3 (integration — `tests/integration/` lane behind `integration-tests` feature; SimClock + SimDriver mean the test runs on macOS in the default `--features integration-tests` lane, no Linux kernel dependency, no `ProcessDriver` cleanup needed).
quality_gates: RED verified at the compile layer: `cargo check -p overdrive-control-plane --tests --features integration-tests` errors with `unknown field clock in ServerConfig` and/or `unknown field tick_cadence in ServerConfig`. Any other compile error (typo, missing fixture import like `SimClock` / `SimDriver` / `SimObservationStore`, missing mod declaration in `tests/integration.rs`) is a step-author bug — fix and re-verify.

No shadow `ServerConfig` type and no test-local fields that would mask the production-side gap: `git grep -n 'struct ServerConfig\|impl ServerConfig\|ServerConfig {' crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/` returns only struct-literal construction sites (no shadow type definitions; no manual `impl` blocks).
No production-code paths touched: `git diff --name-only HEAD` shows only the new test file and the modified `tests/integration.rs` (mod declaration append).
No `#![cfg(...)]` attribute on the new test file (the entrypoint gates the binary; the test is platform-independent because it uses SimDriver).
No new env-var / cwd / process-global mutation introduced — `#[serial_test::serial(env)]` is NOT needed; the test uses tempdir-scoped `data_dir` / `operator_config_dir`.
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer and explicit `--no-verify` rationale citing `.claude/rules/testing.md` §RED scaffolds, naming Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
deliverables: New file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot.rs` with one `#[tokio::test]` named `submitted_job_reaches_running_via_real_server_boot` whose body references `ServerConfig.clock` and `ServerConfig.tick_cadence` directly.
`crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration.rs` updated with `mod convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot;` inside the existing `pub mod job_lifecycle { ... }` block (lines 50-63).
Single commit with `--no-verify` and `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit body cites `.claude/rules/testing.md` §RED scaffolds and names Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Regression test design and §Approved fix items 1-7. Audit existing fixtures: (a) `tests/integration/submit_round_trip.rs:43-103` for the `client_trusting`, `read_ca_from_trust_triple`, and `spawn_server` helper shapes (the new test will inline these or copy-paste verbatim — there is no shared helpers module yet, see `tests/integration/observation_empty_rows.rs:38` comment about the duplication choice). (b) `tests/integration/observation_empty_rows.rs:78-108` for the `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` direct-construction shape (constructs `obs` and `driver` outside the helper so the test can hold a handle to the observation store — same pattern needed here so we can hold the SimClock for `clock.tick(...)`). (c) `tests/integration/job_lifecycle/submit_to_running.rs:33-96` for the `JobSpecInput { id: "payments", replicas: 1, cpu_milli: 100, memory_bytes: 256 * 1024 * 1024 }` shape and the convergence-loop drive pattern. Note that `submit_to_running.rs` is `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` because it uses real `ProcessDriver`; the new test deliberately does NOT carry that gate. RED_ACCEPTANCE — write the test. Imports needed (mirror `observation_empty_rows.rs:18-31`): `std::sync::Arc`, `std::time::Duration`, `overdrive_control_plane::api::{ClusterStatus, AllocStatusResponse, IdempotencyOutcome, SubmitJobRequest, SubmitJobResponse}`, `overdrive_control_plane::{ServerConfig, ServerHandle, run_server_with_obs_and_driver}`, `overdrive_core::aggregate::JobSpecInput`, `overdrive_core::id::NodeId`, `overdrive_core::traits::driver::{Driver, DriverType}`, `overdrive_core::traits::observation_store::ObservationStore`, `overdrive_sim::adapters::clock::SimClock`, `overdrive_sim::adapters::driver::SimDriver`, `overdrive_sim::adapters::observation_store::SimObservationStore`, `tempfile::TempDir`. The reqwest client + trust-triple-read helpers can be inlined verbatim from `submit_round_trip.rs` (same pattern as the existing duplication tolerated across integration tests). RED_UNIT — N/A (no internal helpers introduced). GREEN — SKIPPED in this step. COMMIT — `git commit --no-verify` with body: `RED scaffold per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits. The new acceptance test references \`ServerConfig.clock\` and \`ServerConfig.tick_cadence\` fields that land in Step 01-02 along with the production-side spawn of the convergence tick loop. The compile failure is the RED state; the crafter must not introduce a shadow ServerConfig or comment out the field references. Step-ID: 01-01`. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase` — do NOT edit `execution-log.json` directly. CRITICAL crafter callout: the SimClock method is `tick(&self, duration: Duration)` (sync, returns `()`) — it is NOT `advance(...).await` (the user-facing prompt wrote `clock.advance(...).await` but that method exists on `SimObservationStore`, not on `SimClock`). Verified at `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/clock.rs:55` (`pub fn tick(&self, duration: Duration)`).  Use the literal call `clock.tick(Duration::from_millis(100));` followed by `tokio::task::yield_now().await;` per loop iteration.

steps.name: GREEN cohesive fix — Option B2 broker-driven §18 wiring with all 7 production edits
steps.description: Apply the RCA §Approved fix Option B2 in a single cohesive commit. SEVEN production changes plus one Cargo.toml addition. (1) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs` — `ServerConfig` (currently lines 137-188): add `tick_cadence: Duration` field defaulting to `overdrive_control_plane::reconciler_runtime::DEFAULT_TICK_CADENCE` (100ms, defined at `reconciler_runtime.rs:144`), and `clock: Arc<dyn overdrive_core::traits::clock::Clock>` defaulting to `Arc::new(overdrive_host::SystemClock)`. Update the `Default` impl (currently `lib.rs:168-188`) so existing test fixtures using `ServerConfig { bind, data_dir, operator_config_dir, allow_no_cgroups: true }` (without rest-pattern) continue to compile if they DO use rest-pattern, OR — if they don't — add explicit `tick_cadence: DEFAULT_TICK_CADENCE` and `clock: Arc::new(SystemClock)` to each call site. Audit needed: `git grep -n 'ServerConfig {' crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/` to enumerate construction sites (≈8 sites across `submit_round_trip.rs`, `describe_round_trip.rs`, `idempotent_resubmit.rs`, `concurrent_submit_toctou.rs`, `server_lifecycle.rs`, `observation_empty_rows.rs`, `tls_bootstrap.rs`, `libsql_isolation.rs`, plus the new `convergence_loop_spawned_in_production_boot.rs`). Add `..Default::default()` rest pattern to each call site that doesn't already use it, OR set both fields explicitly. The narrower change is `..Default::default()` rest pattern. (2) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs` — `ServerHandle` (currently lines 196-221): add `convergence_task: tokio::task::JoinHandle<()>` and `convergence_shutdown: tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken` fields. Update `shutdown(...)` (currently lines 213-220) to: `self.convergence_shutdown.cancel(); let _ = self.convergence_task.await; self.inner.graceful_shutdown(Some(drain_deadline)); let _ = self.server_task.await;`. The cancel-then-await convergence-first ordering matters because the convergence task holds `Arc<dyn Driver>` references; reversing this risks reconciler tasks attempting work against axum-shutting-down state. (3) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs` — `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` (currently lines 284-403): between `AppState::new(store, obs, runtime, driver)` (line 354) and the listener bind block (lines 371-375), spawn the convergence loop. Code shape: `let convergence_shutdown = tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken::new(); let convergence_task = { let state = state.clone(); let clock = config.clock.clone(); let cadence = config.tick_cadence; let token = convergence_shutdown.clone(); tokio::spawn(async move { let mut tick_n: u64 = 0; loop { let now = clock.now(); let deadline = now + cadence; let pending = state.runtime.broker_mut().drain_pending(); for eval in pending { if let Err(e) = run_convergence_tick(&state, &eval.target, now, tick_n, deadline).await { tracing::warn!(target: "overdrive::reconciler", ?e, "tick error"); } } tick_n = tick_n.saturating_add(1); tokio::select! { _ = clock.sleep(cadence) => {}, _ = token.cancelled() => break, } } }) };`. Update the final `Ok(ServerHandle { inner: axum_handle, server_task })` to `Ok(ServerHandle { inner: axum_handle, server_task, convergence_task, convergence_shutdown })`. (4) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs` — `submit_job` (currently lines 99-156): after the `PutOutcome::Inserted` and `PutOutcome::KeyExists` branches (line 130 onward), but BEFORE the `Ok(...)` returns on the `Inserted` and byte-equal `Unchanged` paths (NOT on the 409 Conflict path), call `state.runtime.broker_mut().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle").map_err(|e| ControlPlaneError::internal("reconciler name", e))?, target: TargetResource::new(format!("job/{}", job.id)).map_err(|e| ControlPlaneError::internal("target resource", e))? });`. The `Conflict` branch deliberately does NOT enqueue (a 409 means no intent change, so no convergence work is needed). Same shape on `stop_job` (currently lines 267-297) — after the `put_if_absent` succeeds (whether `Stopped` or `AlreadyStopped`), enqueue an Evaluation with the same `(reconciler="job-lifecycle", target="job/{job_id}")` shape so the convergence loop reconciles the stop intent against the running allocs. (5) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` — `run_convergence_tick` (currently lines 164-213): inside the `for name in &registered { ... }` loop (line 175), after `action_shim::dispatch(actions, ...)` succeeds (line 209), if `actions.len() > 0` (i.e. the reconciler emitted work), self-re-enqueue: `if !actions.is_empty() { state.runtime.broker_mut().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: name.clone(), target: target.clone() }); }`. CRITICAL ORDERING: capture `actions.len()` (or clone the slice's emptiness) BEFORE consuming the `actions` Vec into `dispatch`; the current code passes `actions` by-value into `action_shim::dispatch` (line 209), so the post-dispatch `if !actions.is_empty()` check would not compile. Either: (a) capture `let has_work = !actions.is_empty();` before the dispatch call, then branch on `has_work` after, OR (b) change the `dispatch` signature to accept `&[Action]` if appropriate. Option (a) is the lower-blast-radius choice. (6) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` — replace the aspirational docstring at lines 155-158 (`/// Production wiring spawns a tokio task that calls this in a loop with `clock.sleep(tick_cadence)` between invocations.`) with a docstring naming the actual call site: `/// Spawned by [`crate::run_server_with_obs_and_driver`] as a tokio task that drains the [`crate::eval_broker::EvaluationBroker`] each tick (`config.tick_cadence`, default [`DEFAULT_TICK_CADENCE`]) and dispatches one call per pending [`crate::eval_broker::Evaluation`]. Shutdown ordering: [`crate::ServerHandle::shutdown`] cancels the convergence task FIRST (via `convergence_shutdown` token), awaits its join, THEN triggers axum graceful shutdown. The reverse ordering risks reconciler tasks holding `Arc<dyn Driver>` while axum-shutting-down state is accessed.`. (7) `crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/serve.rs` — `pub async fn run(args: ServeArgs) -> Result<ServeHandle, CliError>` (currently lines 101-126): the `ServerConfig { ... }` construction at lines 104-109 must explicitly pass `clock: Arc::new(overdrive_host::SystemClock)` and `tick_cadence: DEFAULT_TICK_CADENCE` (or use `..Default::default()` rest pattern if `Default` impl is updated to carry those values). Also: `crates/overdrive-cli/Cargo.toml` may need to add `overdrive-host.workspace = true` if not already a runtime dep (per CLAUDE.md "Repository structure" `overdrive-host` is the only crate permitted to instantiate `SystemClock`). PLUS workspace + crate Cargo.toml: workspace root `Cargo.toml` `[workspace.dependencies]` block adds `tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["rt"] }` (verify: `git grep '^tokio-util' Cargo.toml` returns no matches today). `crates/overdrive-control-plane/Cargo.toml` adds `tokio-util.workspace = true` under `[dependencies]` (currently absent). PLUS broker-mutability adapter (recommended path, see roadmap notes): change `ReconcilerRuntime::broker` to either return `MutexGuard<'_, EvaluationBroker>` from a `parking_lot::Mutex<EvaluationBroker>` field, OR introduce both `broker(&self) -> ReadGuard` (for `cluster_status` counters read) and `broker_mut(&self) -> WriteGuard` (for handler enqueue + spawn-loop drain + reconciler self-re-enqueue). The `parking_lot::Mutex` choice is the simplest: change `broker: EvaluationBroker` field at `reconciler_runtime.rs:57` to `broker: parking_lot::Mutex<EvaluationBroker>`, update `new()` (line 83) to wrap, and add a `pub fn broker(&self) -> parking_lot::MutexGuard<'_, EvaluationBroker>` accessor (replacing the current `pub const fn broker(&self) -> &EvaluationBroker`). Update `cluster_status` (`handlers.rs:326-340`) to call `state.runtime.broker().counters()` against the guard (reads still work, no API drift on `counters()`). Per `.claude/rules/development.md` §Concurrency & async: "Never hold a lock across `.await`" — the spawn loop holds the guard only long enough to call `drain_pending()` into a `Vec<Evaluation>`, then drops it before the per-eval `.await` calls; `submit_job` / `stop_job` hold the guard only for the `submit()` call. Test verification within this commit: Step 01-01's regression test transitions from RED (compile-failed against current main) to GREEN (compile + pass) without modification. The 5 existing real-server tests (`submit_round_trip`, `describe_round_trip`, `idempotent_resubmit`, `concurrent_submit_toctou`, `server_lifecycle`) plus `observation_empty_rows`, `tls_bootstrap`, `libsql_isolation` continue to pass — they go through `run_server` (which uses real `SystemClock` + real `ProcessDriver`), the convergence loop spawns with `allow_no_cgroups = true` causing the loop to be a no-op in their case (no broker submissions), but a real submit DOES enqueue and the broker drains it. Per RCA §Risk: any test that submits a job through `run_server` will see SimDriver / ProcessDriver activity post-submit; the test must either (a) shut down before convergence has time to act (their existing 2-second drain deadline plus shutdown ordering should handle this — convergence_shutdown cancels FIRST, draining stops mid-cycle), or (b) be migrated to inject a `SimClock` to control timing. The audit step within this PR is: run `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` on macOS, then `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` inside Lima VM, AND `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` on Linux CI. Any test failure is addressed in this commit (NOT masked, NOT ignored). Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: all 7 production edits + the workspace dep + the broker-mutability adapter land in one commit. No deprecation, no shadow type, no two-phase rollout. Commit message: `fix(control-plane): spawn convergence tick loop in production boot with broker-driven §18 wiring (Option B2)` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. Normal pre-commit hooks apply (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Production changes (single cohesive commit) — exactly the 7 edits enumerated in the description: (1) `ServerConfig` adds `clock: Arc<dyn Clock>` and `tick_cadence: Duration` fields with `Default` impl carrying `Arc::new(SystemClock)` and `DEFAULT_TICK_CADENCE`. (2) `ServerHandle` adds `convergence_task` + `convergence_shutdown` fields; `shutdown(...)` cancel-await-axum-await order. (3) `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` spawns the convergence loop between `AppState::new` (line 354) and listener bind (line 371). (4) `submit_job` (lines 99-156) and `stop_job` (lines 267-297) call `broker_mut().submit(Evaluation { reconciler: ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle")?, target: TargetResource::new(format!("job/{job_id}"))? })` after IntentStore writes succeed. (5) `run_convergence_tick` (lines 164-213) self-re-enqueues when `actions` is non-empty. (6) Aspirational docstring at lines 155-158 replaced with one naming `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` and `ServerHandle::convergence_task`. (7) `crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/serve.rs:104-109` constructs `ServerConfig` with `clock: Arc::new(overdrive_host::SystemClock)` (or via `..Default::default()`). PLUS workspace `Cargo.toml` adds `tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["rt"] }` to `[workspace.dependencies]`; `crates/overdrive-control-plane/Cargo.toml` adds `tokio-util.workspace = true`. PLUS broker-mutability adapter: `ReconcilerRuntime::broker` returns `parking_lot::MutexGuard<'_, EvaluationBroker>` (or equivalent interior-mutability shape — the choice is documented in the commit body). Test verification: (1) Step 01-01's regression test transitions RED → GREEN within this commit without modification. (2) All existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0 (default lane); `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` typechecks on macOS; full integration run inside Lima VM (`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`) exits 0. (3) `cargo xtask dst` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 (the spawn loop runs in the wiring crate `overdrive-control-plane` which is `crate_class = adapter-host` — dst-lint scans only `core` crates, so direct `Clock::now`/`Clock::sleep` via the injected trait is allowed there). (4) `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on `overdrive-control-plane`, `overdrive-cli`. (5) Mutation gate (post-GREEN, run with `run_in_background: true` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing exception): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` reports kill rate ≥80%; the new test's two assertions kill the mutations (a) deletion of `tokio::spawn(...)` block (Root Cause A), (b) deletion of the `submit_job` / `stop_job` `broker_mut().submit(...)` call (Root Cause B), (c) replacing `dispatched.saturating_add(drained.len() as u64)` with `dispatched` (Root Cause C — broker counter never advances). Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Single-cut migration: `git grep -E '#\[deprecated|#\[allow\(deprecated\]|TODO.*remove' crates/overdrive-control-plane/ crates/overdrive-cli/` returns no matches in the diff; no shim/proxy/old-path-with-feature-flag. Commit message: `fix(control-plane): spawn convergence tick loop in production boot with broker-driven §18 wiring (Option B2)` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`). Post-GREEN smoke check: `git diff --stat HEAD~1` shows the expected breadth — `lib.rs`, `handlers.rs`, `reconciler_runtime.rs` in control-plane; `commands/serve.rs` in cli; `Cargo.toml` (workspace) and `Cargo.toml` (control-plane); plus the test file from 01-01's prior commit.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Symptom (closure) — the convergence loop is now spawned by `run_server_with_obs_and_driver`, the broker is drained each tick, dispatched evaluations advance allocations from `Pending` to `Running`, and `cluster_status.broker.dispatched` advances under steady-state traffic. Step 01-01's `info.broker.dispatched >= 1` assertion passes against the real bound HTTPS listener.

RCA §5 Whys WHY 5 (root cause closure for all three compounding causes) — Root A (production omission) closed by the `tokio::spawn` of the convergence task in `run_server_with_obs_and_driver`. Root B (handlers don't enqueue) closed by `broker_mut().submit(Evaluation { ... })` in `submit_job` and `stop_job`. Root C (no automated gate on `broker.dispatched`) closed by Step 01-01's regression test at `info.broker.dispatched >= 1`.

RCA §Approved fix items 1-7 — all 7 production edits land in this commit (ServerConfig fields, ServerHandle fields + shutdown ordering, spawn block, submit/stop enqueue, run_convergence_tick self-re-enqueue, docstring fix, CLI wiring). Plus workspace tokio-util dep and broker-mutability adapter as architectural prerequisites flagged in the roadmap notes.

Step 01-01's regression test flips RED → GREEN within this commit without modification (the `unknown field` compile errors close on the `ServerConfig` field additions; the `broker.dispatched >= 1` runtime assertion passes because the spawn + handler-enqueue + drain-and-dispatch chain is now live; the `Running` runtime assertion passes because SimDriver records the alloc on the dispatched StartAllocation action).

All existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` typechecks on macOS; the full integration run passes inside Lima VM. Per RCA §Risk: the 5 existing real-server tests have been audited against the new spawn behaviour and any that misbehave have been adjusted in this commit (NOT masked).

Reconciler purity preserved per `.claude/rules/development.md` §Reconciler I/O — `run_convergence_tick` continues to construct `TickContext { now, tick, deadline }` from the runtime-injected `Clock`, never `Instant::now()`. The spawn loop runs in the wiring crate (`overdrive-control-plane` is `adapter-host` per `crate_class`); direct `clock.now()` / `clock.sleep(...)` calls via the injected `Arc<dyn Clock>` are allowed there. `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: all 7 production edits + workspace dep + broker-mutability adapter land in one commit. No `#[deprecated]` aliases, no shadow `ServerConfig` types, no feature-flagged old path, no two-phase rollout. `git grep -E '#\[deprecated|TODO.*remove|backwards-compat' crates/overdrive-control-plane/` returns no matches in the diff.

`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green on `overdrive-control-plane` and `overdrive-cli`. Typed errors per `.claude/rules/development.md` (no new `eyre::Report` on library boundaries; the `ReconcilerName::new(...)?` and `TargetResource::new(...)?` failures map through `ControlPlaneError::internal(...)` which already exists; no new `unsafe` blocks).

Mutation gate met: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` reports ≥80% kill rate. The new test's two assertions kill the three Root Cause classes' mutations: (a) deleting the `tokio::spawn(async move { ... })` block, (b) deleting the handler-side `broker_mut().submit(...)` calls in submit_job AND stop_job, (c) replacing the `dispatched` accumulator increment in `EvaluationBroker::drain_pending` with a no-op. Plus the self-re-enqueue mutation (deleting the `if !actions.is_empty() { broker_mut().submit(...); }` block in `run_convergence_tick`) is killed by the regression test reaching `Running` rather than stalling at `Pending` after the first dispatch. If any mutation survives, add a concrete test case that disambiguates it — NOT weaken existing assertions.

Workspace + crate Cargo.toml updates land: `[workspace.dependencies]` adds `tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["rt"] }` (verify: `git grep -n 'tokio-util' Cargo.toml` shows the new entry); `crates/overdrive-control-plane/Cargo.toml` adds `tokio-util.workspace = true` under `[dependencies]`; `crates/overdrive-cli/Cargo.toml` adds `overdrive-host.workspace = true` under `[dependencies]` if not already present.

Broker-mutability adapter landed: `ReconcilerRuntime` field at `reconciler_runtime.rs:57` is `broker: parking_lot::Mutex<EvaluationBroker>` (or equivalent); the `broker(...)` accessor returns a guard, the cluster_status counters call works against the guard, and the spawn loop / handler enqueue / self-re-enqueue all hold the guard only for the synchronous broker call (never across `.await`). The choice is documented in the commit body.
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.tier: Tier 3 (integration — `tests/integration/` lane behind `integration-tests` feature). Mutation testing scope: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing.
quality_gates: Workspace compiles green: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck) exits 0.
Default-lane tests green on macOS: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-cli` exits 0.

Step 01-01's regression test transitions RED → GREEN without modification: run `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(submitted_job_reaches_running_via_real_server_boot)'` inside Lima VM and confirm the test passes.

All existing real-server integration tests pass under the new spawn behaviour: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` (Lima VM) exits 0. Per RCA §Risk, any test that fails has been adjusted in this commit (the load-bearing assertion is NOT weakened — typically the fix is to inject a `SimClock` if the test was implicitly relying on `SystemClock` quiescence).

Full workspace integration run green inside Lima VM: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` exits 0 — surfaces any test in any other crate that was indirectly relying on the production-boot omission.
DST run green: `cargo xtask dst` exits 0 (DST instantiates `run_convergence_tick` directly per RCA §Test-coverage gaps; the spawn-loop change is invisible to DST).

dst-lint clean: `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0. The spawn loop's `clock.now()` / `clock.sleep(cadence)` calls are in `overdrive-control-plane` (`adapter-host` class per `crate_class` in Cargo.toml line 21); dst-lint scans only `core` crates per CLAUDE.md "Repository structure". `run_convergence_tick`'s purity discipline (use `tick.now`, not `Instant::now()`) is unaffected.
Clippy clean: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` exits 0 on `overdrive-control-plane`, `overdrive-cli`.

Single-cut migration: `git grep -E '#\[deprecated|#\[allow\(deprecated\]|TODO.*remove|backwards-compat' crates/overdrive-control-plane/ crates/overdrive-cli/` returns no matches in the diff. No shim/proxy/old-path-with-feature-flag.

Mutation gate (run AFTER GREEN with `run_in_background: true` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing exception): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` reports ≥80% kill rate. Expected mutations to be killed: (a) deleting `tokio::spawn(async move { ... })` block in `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` killed by `broker.dispatched >= 1` assertion; (b) deleting `broker_mut().submit(...)` in `submit_job` killed by `broker.dispatched >= 1` AND `Running` assertions; (c) replacing `dispatched.saturating_add(drained.len() as u64)` with `dispatched` in `EvaluationBroker::drain_pending` killed by `broker.dispatched >= 1` assertion; (d) deleting `if !actions.is_empty() { broker_mut().submit(Evaluation { ... }); }` self-re-enqueue in `run_convergence_tick` killed by the `Running` assertion (one-shot dispatch without re-enqueue stalls at `Pending`); (e) reversing the shutdown ordering (axum-first instead of convergence-first) killed by any test that asserts on a clean shutdown including `server_lifecycle.rs`. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the pass terminates regardless of outcome.

Production edit precision: `git diff HEAD~1 -- crates/` shows exactly the 7 production edits enumerated in the description PLUS the broker-mutability adapter PLUS the workspace + crate Cargo.toml additions; no incidental edits, no unrelated refactors, no whitespace-only changes outside the touched line ranges.

Step 01-01's regression test transitions RED → GREEN within this commit without modification.

All pre-existing workspace tests remain GREEN under default lane and integration lane (Lima VM); any test that misbehaves under the new spawn behaviour has been adjusted in this commit (RCA §Risk closure).
steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Approved fix items 1-7, §Risk, and §Test-coverage gaps. Confirm current line numbers in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs` (137-188 for `ServerConfig`, 196-221 for `ServerHandle`, 284-403 for `run_server_with_obs_and_driver`, 354 for `AppState::new`, 371 for listener bind, 400 for axum spawn — these may have drifted slightly; locate by symbol name, not line number). Confirm `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs` line numbers (99-156 for `submit_job`, 267-297 for `stop_job`, 299-343 for `cluster_status`). Confirm `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` line numbers (144 for `DEFAULT_TICK_CADENCE`, 155-158 for the aspirational docstring, 164-213 for `run_convergence_tick`, 121 for `broker(...)` accessor, 57 for the `broker:` field). Confirm broker API: `eval_broker.rs:87` is `pub fn submit(&mut self, eval: Evaluation)` (NOT `enqueue`); `eval_broker.rs:98` is `pub fn drain_pending(&mut self) -> Vec<Evaluation>`; `eval_broker.rs:53-57` shows `Evaluation { reconciler, target }` is a plain struct, NOT `Evaluation::new(...)` constructor. Confirm reconciler name: `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:1062` shows `ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle")` (with hyphen). validation.notes: Roadmap quality gate (6 checks) PASS: (1) AC coupling — refs only pub fields and trait methods (`broker.dispatched`, `AllocStatusRow.state`, `IdempotencyOutcome::Inserted`); (2) decomposition ratio 2 steps / 7 production files = 0.29, well under 2.5; (3) only 2 steps, distinct shapes (RED scaffold / GREEN cohesive fix); (4) both steps have files_to_modify with absolute paths; (5) step IDs match ^\d{2}-\d{2}$; (6) RCA linkage — test_file and scenario_name populated; both steps trace to RCA §Symptom, §5 Whys, §Approved fix items 1-7, and §Regression test design; risk-mitigation explicit per RCA §Risk. Phase 1d reviewer (nw-acceptance-designer-reviewer) skipped: this is a /nw-bugfix flow with no DISTILL artifact to verify scenario coverage against. The user-APPROVED RCA at `docs/feature/fix-convergence-loop-not-spawned/bugfix-rca.md` is the load-bearing specification. Two architectural concerns flagged in roadmap notes for crafter visibility: (1) RCA referred to `broker.enqueue(...)` but the actual API is `broker.submit(...)`; (2) `broker()` returns `&EvaluationBroker` but `submit`/`drain_pending` need `&mut`, requiring an interior-mutability adapter — recommended path documented as `parking_lot::Mutex<EvaluationBroker>`. Proceeding to Phase 2 execution.
